text: Databases are essential for storing and organising information efficiently. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL databases. SQL databases, like MySQL, use structured tables with rows and columns, making them ideal for complex queries and relationships between data. NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, are more flexible, handling unstructured data like documents or graphs. Data modeling involves designing how information is structured, whether in fixed tables or flexible formats. Queries allow you to retrieve specific data, with SQL using commands like SELECT and NoSQL using its own syntax. Indexes speed up these queries by acting as shortcuts to data, reducing search time. Choosing the right type depends on your project’s needs for structure, scalability, or flexibility.
